title: rootless libpod (v3.4.4) does not always configure an image store
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

A libpod-using program (written in Go) can only call `(*Runtime).LibimageRuntime().LookupImage()` the first time it runs.  Later executions of the program panic with a nil pointer dereference.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. In rootless mode, call `podman, err := libpod.NewRuntime(ctx, ...)`.

2. Call `_, _, err = podman.LibimageRuntime().LookupImage(...)`.

3. Run the program a second time.

**Describe the results you received:**

The LookupImage() call works during the program's first execution, but panics the second (and later) times the program runs.

**Describe the results you expected:**

LookupImage() should consistently succeed or consistently fail, independent of previous program executions.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

`(Runtime).libimageRuntime` is only sometimes assigned (by calling `(*Runtime).configureStore()`): Namely, when executed with `CAP_SYS_ADMIN` or when `runtimeAliveFile` needs to be re-created.  In rootless mode, when `runtimeAliveFile` already exists, `configureStore()` is never called and so `libimageRuntime` remains nil.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
ersion:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.17.5
Built:        Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Rootless mode on a physical x86_64 system.

comments.body:
I am not sure if we support using libpod as library, at least it does not have a stable interface so you could run into breaking changes on every update.
There is some special code in https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/rootless/rootless_linux.c which should make podman join the user ns without having to rexec.
I think you need to call this https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/905d31ddd358bb6c40082c391102bbf92a04fad6/cmd/podman/root.go#L263-L275

If we were to support Libpod outside of Podman, I think we'd have to do it root-only, because there's far too much setup code for rootless, and it's very difficult to disentangle from the structure of Podman itself.
Or at least say it has to be used as root inside of a user namespace.

Thank you for the feedback.

From my perspective, it is fine if libpod is not supported, or only supported as root -- I can call out to the system podman executable from my program.  It would be helpful if any such limitation is documented in a libpod package-level documentation comment.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
